leadText: Corrosive Gas (Japanese: ふしょくガス Corrosive Gas) is a non-damaging Poison-type move introduced during Generation VIII, in The Isle of Armor.

sections.title: Effect
sections.text: Contents 1 Effect 1.1 Generation VIII 1.1.1 Pokémon Brilliant Diamond and Shining Pearl 1.2 Generation IX Effect [ edit | edit source ] This move effect may be in need of research. Reason: Does Corrosive Gas affect Arceus 's Plates or Ogerpon 's Masks? You can discuss this on the talk page. Corrosive Gas removes the targets' held items for the rest of the battle (even if switched out). Held items removed by Corrosive Gas cannot be recovered by Recycle or Harvest . Corrosive Gas does not affect an item held by Pokémon with the Sticky Hold Ability, or if it hits a Pokémon's substitute . It also does not affect a Griseous Orb , Drive , Memory , Rusted Sword , or Rusted Shield held by Giratina , Genesect , Silvally , Zacian , or Zamazenta , respectively. Generation VIII [ edit | edit source ] Pokémon Brilliant Diamond and Shining Pearl [ edit | edit source ] Corrosive Gas cannot be selected in a battle. Generation IX [ edit | edit source ] As no available Pokémon can learn Corrosive Gas, it can only be used through Metronome .

sections.title: Description
sections.text: Description [ edit | edit source ] Games Description Sw Sh LA The user surrounds everything around it with highly acidic gas and melts away items they hold. BD SP This move can't be used. It's recommended that this move is forgotten. Once forgotten, this move can't be remembered. S V The user surrounds everything around it with highly acidic gas and melts away items held by other Pokémon.

sections.title: Trivia
sections.text: Trivia [ edit | edit source ] This move's effect is the same as the secondary effect of Knock Off from Generation V onwards. Corrosive Gas is the only Move Tutor move introduced in The Isle of Armor to not be learnable by any Pokémon in Pokémon Scarlet and Violet .
